incidents.victim: Aqua Security, Checkmarx, LiteLLM, Telnyx (software supply chain)
incidents.vector: Compromised GitHub Actions and PyPI packages via stolen maintainer credentials, deploying credential-harvesting malware through trusted open-source tooling including Trivy, KICS, LiteLLM, and Telnyx SDK.
incidents.impact: Estimated 300 GB of credentials stolen from hundreds of CI/CD pipelines; 100+ million monthly downloads of affected packages exposed; CISA added CVE-2026-33634 to KEV catalog with April 8 remediation deadline. Group partnered with Vect ransomware-as-a-service operation and distributed affiliate keys to ~300,000 BreachForums users.

incidents.vector: Compromised personal email account of FBI Director; leaked personal photographs, emails from 2010-2019, and documents via newly created websites after FBI seized four prior domains.
incidents.impact: Historical personal email data exposed; FBI confirmed no government information compromised. US government offered $10 million bounty on group members; four domains seized. Group claimed retaliation for FBI domain seizures and the sinking of Iranian Navy frigate IRIS Dena.

incidents.vector: Targeted Microsoft environment endpoint management systems at the medical device manufacturer; assessed as opportunistic compromise rather than systematic healthcare sector targeting.
incidents.impact: Network disruption affecting medical device manufacturing infrastructure. Company began restoring IT systems as of March 25. CISA issued advisory on March 18.

incidents.vector: Published malicious PyPI package versions (4.87.1, 4.87.2) using stolen credentials; novel steganography technique embedding payloads in WAV audio files to blend with Telnyx voice/telecom API context.
incidents.impact: 670,000+ monthly downloads affected; platform-specific credential harvesters deployed on Windows, Linux, and macOS. PyPI quarantined both versions within hours. Last known safe version: 4.87.0.

incidents.vector: Attackers claimed to have used credentials stolen from the TeamPCP supply chain campaign to breach internal systems; offering access for sale via encrypted messaging.
incidents.impact: Claimed 3 GB breach including internal code repositories, AWS/Azure configurations, GitHub Enterprise user data, and employee information. Breach unconfirmed — AstraZeneca has not issued a public statement.

incidents.vector: Self-propagating worm (CanisterWorm) spreading through exposed Docker APIs, Kubernetes clusters, and Redis servers; executes data destruction on systems matching Iran timezone or Farsi language settings.
incidents.impact: Wiper destroys data on all nodes in compromised Kubernetes clusters; uses Internet Computer Protocol canisters for command and control. Shares infrastructure with the broader TeamPCP supply chain campaign.

incidents.vector: Active exploitation of CVE-2026-33017 (CVSS 9.8) — unauthenticated code injection in Langflow API endpoint allowing arbitrary Python execution via exec() without sandboxing.
incidents.impact: 1,000+ exploitation attempts across multiple regions within 24 hours of disclosure; attackers deploying info stealers, reverse shells, and cryptominers. API keys for OpenAI, Anthropic, and AWS extracted. CISA added to KEV catalog March 25; remediated in Langflow 1.9.0.

incidents.victim: Signal and secure messaging users (government officials, military, journalists)
incidents.vector: Operatives posed as technical support staff, tricked victims into sharing security codes used to link attacker-controlled devices to victim Signal accounts for real-time message interception.
incidents.impact: Thousands of accounts compromised; FBI and French authorities issued security alerts. Dutch intelligence agencies had issued similar warnings earlier in March. Ongoing campaign targeting high-value individuals.

incidents.vector: Ransomware attack shut down all public municipal services except emergency systems in the city of 30,000 residents.
incidents.impact: Complete municipal service outage; officials considering declaring state of emergency to receive state and federal assistance.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack limiting access to internal systems; likely ransomware but not officially confirmed.
incidents.impact: Limited access to internal IT systems affecting metro operations.

incidents.victim: P3 Global Intel (law enforcement tip platform)
incidents.vector: Exploited vulnerability in the P3 Global Intel crime tip management platform used by thousands of law enforcement agencies.
incidents.impact: 93+ GB of confidential law enforcement tip data exfiltrated, including inadequately encrypted informant information. Data shared with journalists and DDoSecrets. Dubbed 'BlueLeaks 2.0.'

incidents.vector: Security breach of employee benefits and retirement funds management platform; breach traced to December 2025 but reported in March 2026.
incidents.impact: Social Security numbers and health plan information of approximately 2.7 million Americans stolen.

incidents.vector: Initial access via compromised employee laptop; lateral movement to company cryptocurrency wallets, customer records, and gift card inventory systems.
incidents.impact: Cryptocurrency stolen from company wallets, customer data exfiltrated, and attempted manipulation of gift card inventory.

incidents.vector: Supply chain intrusion compromising Electron apps and browser plugins to collect private keys and seed phrases from cryptocurrency wallets.
incidents.impact: $7 million in cryptocurrency stolen from affected wallet users.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack crippled IT network; breathalyzer devices installed in vehicles unable to connect to servers to confirm test results.
incidents.impact: Drivers under court DUI orders unable to start their vehicles for at least two days due to failed server connectivity for breath test confirmation.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack forced cancellation of driver's license appointments across the territory.
incidents.impact: Government service disruption affecting driver's license processing for Puerto Rico residents.

incidents.vector: Ransomware attack targeting port operational technology and IT systems.
incidents.impact: Disruption to port operations at a major Spanish fishing infrastructure facility.

incidents.vector: Cyber breach affecting internal ministry systems; details of intrusion method not publicly disclosed.
incidents.impact: Internal systems compromised at the Netherlands Ministry of Finance.

incidents.vector: Same Iran-linked group that breached FBI Director Patel's email leaked alleged personal information of Lockheed Martin defense company officials.
incidents.impact: Personal information of defense contractor employees exposed publicly.

incidents.vector: Exploited flash loan mechanism to extract funds from the decentralized finance protocol.
incidents.impact: $3.6 million in crypto-assets stolen; this is the platform's second hack following a $27 million loss in September.
